verdict: 2026-07-19 09:46 PDT — The phased Table P redesign is mechanically coherent, and schema plus the named semantic check now own the agreed row/catalog invariants. Two one-way contract gaps remain: the canonical plan does not identify the exact units an engine must run, and the normalized patch representation cannot replay every consult edit it claims to support.

findings.title: Canonical rows price units without identifying them
findings.claim: The artifact stores aggregate group and unit counts, but not file identities, chunk boundaries, or the units selected by light-depth sampling. Consequently, the future engine cannot “execute exactly that artifact”: it must rediscover chunks and choose sampled units, allowing the same approved plan to produce different coverage.
findings.alternative: Give every file/chunk a stable unit ID in unitMap and have each row record its ordered resolved unit IDs—or its explicitly selected sample. A pinned deterministic sampling algorithm is acceptable if its complete inputs are stored in the artifact.
findings.win: Eliminates engine-side scheduling judgment, makes approved coverage reproducible, and lets coverage accounting name exactly what ran and what was omitted.

findings.title: Patch records are not complete edit representations
findings.claim: A patch records only selector, operation, and depth, while a row also contains units, pricing, omissionRisk, and why. An added row or a direct consult edit to those fields cannot be reconstructed from the patch without fresh judgment. File-level exclude/only globs likewise cannot alter members of an aggregate group row. The schema also accepts incoherent forms such as add with null depth or remove with a non-null depth. Thus “every edit” and replayability are stronger than the model actually supports.
findings.alternative: Use a discriminated patch union: remove/restrict carry selectors only; set-depth carries a required depth; add/replace carries a complete row-intent payload from which pricing is deterministically derived. Either apply file globs before unit-map compilation or represent unit membership so patches can address it. Reject invalid operation/payload combinations in the schema.
findings.win: Makes every approved edit replayable without inference, removes malformed patch states, and gives CLI and consult changes one genuinely complete contract.
